title: Process-first target contract for Client and Family Context Foundation

authority.route_evolution_policy: No new route or screen split is authorized by this contract.
out_of_scope: new product scope outside True-UX route evolution authority

representative_implementation_surfaces.reason: Client and family context should begin from a compact entry surface, not isolated detail pages.
Existing screenshot audit flags this as long-screen debt and it is client-visibility-sensitive.
Family member context management has DB-backed service and negative proof seams.
Entity context has DB-backed table proof and tenant/payload visibility boundaries.
Entity registration has wizard validation and no-partial-row proof seams.
Wealth context must remain context review, not readiness or release proof.
Existing screenshot audit flags this as long-screen debt and process-readiness overclaim risk.

supporting_surfaces_not_first_slice.reason: Profile save/reload proof exists; use as supporting source for area entry and QA rather than duplicating implementation in the first slice.
Relationship mapping route exists; deeper process mutation proof can remain a later focused slice.
Entity detail can project client-safe object context; primary implementation should first stabilize queue and step boundaries.

role_guard_contract.route_shell_access: Route visibility never grants mutation, payload or release authority.
principal_and_family_cfo: may view client-safe family and entity context where sensitivity permits
may submit scoped profile, family-member and entity intake changes where the service allows it
must not see internal-only rows or hidden row counts
must not unlock advice, evidence sufficiency, export or client visibility by intake action
advisor_and_compliance: may inspect fuller context where role and tenant scope permit
must still respect sensitivity classification and payload projection
must not treat context review as compliance release or client acceptance
restricted_client_roles: must receive hidden payload mode for restricted or internal-only family/entity rows
must not infer hidden row names, counts or details
cross_tenant_or_wrong_actor: must be denied before payload render or mutation
sensitive denied mutation attempts should be auditable

required_response_flags: mutated: false for denied or invalid requests
forbidden_client_fields: hidden row count for restricted client roles
evidence sufficiency result from context-only action
release or export status from intake-only action
payload_visibility_contract.acceptance: Every UI/API proof must show whether the payload is full, redacted or hidden and must avoid client visibility overclaim.

audit_evidence_contract.failure_mode: Invalid or unauthorized profile, family or entity updates fail closed with no partial row and no client release.
audit_evidence_contract.proof_rule: Every process moved beyond partial status must name positive and negative proof refs in the P0 matrix.

process_contracts.service_boundary: client context route shell plus visibilityEngine projection
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Client/family area entry shows tenant, actor, next family-context action and client-safe projection status.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Area entry does not imply profile completion, evidence sufficiency, advice release, export readiness or client acceptance.

process_contracts.service_boundary: demo tenant context and existing tenant setup seams
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Client context surfaces resolve tenant context and show it as precondition.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: EPIC-07 does not create production tenant onboarding, IdP or schema replacement.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Profile save or submit for review reloads from DB and returns noClientRelease.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Invalid profile or wrong-tenant actor fails closed with no mutation success and no payload disclosure.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Family member rows are tenant-scoped, DB-backed and editable where role and object scope permit.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Wrong object, wrong tenant or restricted role cannot mutate or infer hidden payload.

process_contracts.service_boundary: relationship route context and wealth map context
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Relationship map context supports family understanding and routes to next context action.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Relationship mapping does not approve downstream action readiness, advice, release or export.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Entity list and wizard are DB-backed, tenant-scoped and reloadable.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Invalid submissions do not create partial rows; restricted roles cannot infer internal-only entities.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Wealth map and action board summarize context and one next step.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Context review cannot mark readiness, evidence sufficiency, advice release, export approval or client visibility.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Sensitivity classification produces full, redacted or hidden payload modes for permitted roles.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Restricted client roles cannot view or infer internal-only family/entity context and no manual override exists.

visual_acceptance.rule: If a target screen is changed visually, capture a 1440x1000 screenshot and assert no incoherent overlap. Prefer no page scroll for compact entry surfaces; otherwise record an explicit exception with reason.
visual_acceptance.forbidden_visual_substitute: No spec panels, route labels, annotation rails or report-only UI may be added to satisfy this contract.

matrix_update_rule.forbidden_overclaim: Do not mark EPIC-07 complete unless every retained process step has route/source/API proof, positive proof, negative proof and audit/recovery proof.
